episode_summary: A live-tour compilation episode featuring the gang (with guest Buttons) spins through wartime paranoia, language quirks, sports oddities, and pop-culture trivia, including a WWI conspiracy list, trampoline etymology and toddler-inspired robot football, a Danish retitling of Fahrenheit 451, and a guest theory that aliens are time-traveling humans. Along the way they detour into bananas, Disney originals, nominative determinism, and assorted tour anecdotes.

facts.fact: Okay, my fact this week is that during World War I, a popular conspiracy theory was that German blackmailers had drawn up a list of 47,000 highly placed English perverts.
Toddlers make quite good football coaches, soccer coaches. It turns out.
My fact this week is that Ray Bradbury's 451 Fahrenheit has been translated into over 30 languages under the same title, except in Denmark, where it was published as 233 Celsius.
Aliens are more likely to be us time traveling back from the distant future than they are to be little creatures from another planet.
